# Data Processing Agreement (DPA)

_UK GDPR required document template. Replace all [placeholders] with your organisation details before publication._

**Source:** `External - Customer-Facing Documents/Data Processing Agreement (DPA).docx`

---

Data Processing Agreement

## Uk Gdpr

Parties and scope

Agreement

This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the agreement between the Customer ("Controller") and [Company Name] ("Processor") for [Product Name] services in the United Kingdom.

[Product Name] is offered exclusively in the United Kingdom (England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland). Customer data is processed and stored in Microsoft Azure UK South. [Company Name] does not offer multi-region tenancy, data residency selection, or services outside the UK.

Processor shall process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including this DPA and the applicable order.

This DPA is governed by the laws of England and Wales; courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction (subject to Legal review before execution).

Processing details

Subject matter and duration

Processing of workspace personal data for the subscription term and limited post-termination wind-down per Retention & Deletion Policy.

[Product Name] is a workspace and productivity platform. It is not a medical device, clinical system, or regulated health record. Users must not rely on [Product Name] for diagnosis, treatment, or clinical decision-making. AI outputs are assistive drafts only.

Categories of data and subjects

Category

Examples

Data subjects

Identity & access

Name, email, user ID, roles

Customer users and admins

Workspace content

Notes, tasks, attachments, teams

Customer users

Technical

Logs, IP, device metadata

Customer users

AI-derived

Summaries, transcripts user saves

Customer users

Nature and purpose

Hosting, storage, backup, and recovery in Azure UK South

Collaboration features, search, notifications, administration

Optional AI processing via Azure OpenAI (UK South) — in-memory; no prompt storage

Security monitoring, audit logging, and support

Processor obligations

Security and confidentiality

Implement measures in Annex II (Security Overview) appropriate to risk

Ensure personnel confidentiality and training

Subprocessors only per Subprocessor List with equivalent protections

Assist with data subject requests and DPIAs within reasonable timelines

Notify Controller without undue delay of personal data breaches

International transfers

Primary processing remains in the UK. Any subprocessor transfer outside the UK is covered by UK IDTA / Addendum or adequacy decision, documented in the Subprocessor List.

No multi-region routing or foreign residency options are provided.

Deletion and audit

Return and deletion

On termination, Controller may export data via standard export tools. Processor deletes production copies within 90 days unless law requires retention.

Backup media overwritten per Backup & Recovery Policy.

Audit rights

Processor provides SOC-aligned summaries and security whitepaper annually. On-site audit may be agreed for enterprise tiers with 30 days' notice, subject to confidentiality.

_Template — customize confidentiality and ownership statements for your organisation before distribution._
